seoTitle: Memsensing Authorized Distributor | MEMS Sensors | LiTong Electronics
seoDescription: Authorized distributor of Memsensing MEMS sensors including microphones, pressure sensors, inertial sensors, and environmental sensors. Comprehensive product selection and technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main MEMS sensor categories offered by Memsensing?
faqs.answer: Memsensing offers 4 core MEMS sensor categories: MEMS Microphones for audio applications, Pressure Sensors for altitude and weather monitoring, Inertial Sensors including accelerometers and gyroscopes for motion sensing, and Environmental Sensors for temperature and humidity measurement. Each category offers multiple product series optimized for specific applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any product category to explore detailed specifications, available models, and application guidance.

faqs.question: Is LiTong Electronics an authorized Memsensing distributor?
faqs.answer: Yes, LiTong Electronics is an authorized distributor of Memsensing products. We maintain direct partnerships with Memsensing Microsystems Technology to ensure our customers receive genuine products with full manufacturer warranty and technical support. As an authorized distributor, we have access to Memsensing' complete MEMS sensor portfolio.
faqs.decisionGuide: When purchasing Memsensing products, always verify that your supplier is an authorized distributor to ensure product authenticity and warranty coverage. Contact us for a copy of our authorization certificate.

faqs.question: What are Memsensing' core competitive advantages?
faqs.answer: Memsensing has established several key competitive advantages including proprietary MEMS process technology, vertically integrated manufacturing capabilities, comprehensive product portfolio covering all major MEMS sensor types, strong R&D focus for innovative solutions, and automotive-grade products meeting AEC-Q100 and IATF 16949 standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider Memsensing for projects requiring high-performance MEMS sensors with competitive pricing and reliable supply. Their automotive qualification makes them ideal for demanding applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Memsensing MEMS sensor?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Memsensing MEMS sensor involves evaluating key parameters specific to each sensor type. For microphones, consider sensitivity, SNR, and power consumption. For pressure sensors, evaluate accuracy and pressure range. For inertial sensors, consider measurement range and resolution. For environmental sensors, assess accuracy requirements. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selection guide or contact our FAE team with your specific requirements. We can provide comparison matrices, sample evaluation, and recommendations.

categories.name: MEMS Microphones
categories.description: High-performance MEMS microphones for smartphones, wearables, IoT devices, and voice-controlled applications with excellent SNR and low power consumption.
categories.longDescription: Memsensing MEMS Microphones deliver exceptional audio performance for a wide range of applications. As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance for these advanced microphones. The product range includes both analog and digital microphones with top-port and bottom-port configurations. Featuring high SNR up to 70dB, low power consumption, and excellent sensitivity tolerance, these microphones are ideal for smartphones, TWS earbuds, wearables, smart speakers, and voice-controlled IoT devices. The microphones support various interface options including PDM and I2S digital outputs.
categories.selectionGuide: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal MEMS microphone based on your audio application requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between analog and digital MEMS microphones?
faqs.answer: Analog MEMS microphones output an analog voltage signal proportional to sound pressure, requiring an external ADC for digital processing. Digital MEMS microphones include an integrated ADC and output digital audio via PDM or I2S interface. Digital microphones offer better noise immunity, easier integration with digital systems, and reduced component count.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose digital microphones for new designs to benefit from better noise immunity and easier integration. Analog microphones may be preferred for legacy systems or specific analog signal chain requirements.

faqs.question: What SNR levels are available for Memsensing microphones?
faqs.answer: Memsensing offers microphones with SNR ranging from 58dB to 70dB. Standard models provide 60-64dB SNR suitable for most consumer applications, while premium models offer 66-70dB SNR for high-fidelity audio applications requiring superior sound quality.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select SNR based on your application's audio quality requirements. Higher SNR for premium audio applications, standard SNR for voice recognition and general audio capture.

faqs.question: Are Memsensing microphones suitable for TWS earbuds?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memsensing offers ultra-low power microphones specifically designed for TWS earbuds and wearables. These microphones feature small package sizes (2.75x1.85mm), low current consumption (under 100uA), and excellent RF immunity for reliable operation in compact wireless devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: For TWS applications, select microphones with smallest package size and lowest power consumption. Verify RF immunity specifications for reliable wireless operation.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for digital microphones?
faqs.answer: Memsensing digital microphones support PDM (Pulse Density Modulation) interface, the industry standard for digital MEMS microphones. PDM allows multiple microphones to share a common data line with separate clock signals, simplifying multi-microphone designs for beamforming and noise cancellation applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PDM interface for multi-microphone arrays. Verify your processor supports PDM input or plan for external PDM-to-I2S conversion.

faqs.question: Do Memsensing microphones support voice activation applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memsensing microphones are well-suited for voice activation and voice recognition applications. The high SNR and excellent sensitivity tolerance ensure reliable voice detection across various acoustic environments. Low power modes support always-listening voice activation with minimal battery drain.
faqs.decisionGuide: For voice activation, select microphones with high SNR and good sensitivity tolerance. Consider power consumption for always-listening applications.

faqs.question: What package sizes are available for MEMS microphones?
faqs.answer: Memsensing MEMS microphones are available in industry-standard packages including 3.76x2.95mm, 2.75x1.85mm, and 2.65x1.85mm. The smaller packages are ideal for space-constrained applications like TWS earbuds and wearables.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select package size based on your PCB layout constraints. Smaller packages for space-constrained designs, standard packages for general applications.

products.name: High-Performance Analog MEMS Microphone
products.shortDescription: High-performance analog MEMS microphone with 64dB SNR and -26dBFS sensitivity for premium audio applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MSM261S4030H0R is a high-performance analog MEMS microphone designed for premium audio applications requiring excellent sound quality. Featuring a high SNR of 64dB and sensitivity of -26dBFS, this microphone delivers clear, detailed audio capture for smartphones, tablets, and portable recording devices.

This microphone incorporates advanced MEMS technology with excellent sensitivity tolerance of ±1dB, ensuring consistent performance across production units. The analog output provides wide dynamic range and natural sound characteristics preferred by audio professionals. The microphone operates from a 1.5V to 3.6V supply with low current consumption of 180uA.

The MSM261S4030H0R features a compact 3.76x2.95x1.1mm package with bottom port configuration, suitable for standard PCB mounting. The microphone includes integrated RF shielding for reliable operation in wireless devices and excellent immunity to electromagnetic interference.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MSM261S4030H0R including application guidance, schematic review, and audio performance optimization assistance.

faeReview.author: Steven Chen
faeReview.content: The MSM261S4030H0R has become our go-to analog microphone for premium audio applications. The 64dB SNR delivers excellent audio quality that rivals more expensive microphones from established brands. The ±1dB sensitivity tolerance is impressive - we've seen very consistent performance across thousands of units in production. The analog output provides the natural sound characteristics that audio engineers prefer for high-quality recording applications. Power consumption is as specified, making it suitable for portable devices. The integrated RF shielding works well - we've had no interference issues in smartphones with multiple wireless radios. Overall, a reliable and cost-effective solution for premium audio applications.
highlight: Effective RF shielding for wireless devices

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative with slightly reduced SNR
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for cost-sensitive consumer audio applications where 60dB SNR is acceptable

alternativeParts.reason: Digital output version for easier integration
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for modern digital audio systems requiring PDM interface and better noise immunity

faqs.question: What is the sensitivity tolerance of MSM261S4030H0R?
faqs.answer: The MSM261S4030H0R features excellent sensitivity tolerance of ±1dB, ensuring consistent performance across production units. This tight tolerance simplifies system design and reduces calibration requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring consistent microphone performance across multiple units, the ±1dB tolerance provides excellent production consistency.

faqs.question: Does MSM261S4030H0R require external components?
faqs.answer: The MSM261S4030H0R requires minimal external components - typically just a decoupling capacitor on the power supply. The analog output can connect directly to your audio codec or ADC input. No external biasing or gain components are required.
faqs.decisionGuide: For simplest design, place a 0.1uF decoupling capacitor close to the microphone power pin. Connect output directly to your audio input.

faqs.question: What is the frequency response of MSM261S4030H0R?
faqs.answer: The MSM261S4030H0R has a flat frequency response from 100Hz to 10kHz, suitable for voice and general audio applications. The microphone maintains consistent sensitivity across this range, ensuring accurate sound reproduction.
faqs.decisionGuide: For voice applications, the frequency response is ideal. For extended frequency range requirements, verify specifications meet your needs.

products.name: High-Performance Digital MEMS Microphone
products.shortDescription: High-performance digital MEMS microphone with 64dB SNR and PDM output for modern digital audio systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The MSD3524D1T is a high-performance digital MEMS microphone featuring PDM (Pulse Density Modulation) output for seamless integration with modern digital audio systems. With 64dB SNR and -26dBFS sensitivity, this microphone delivers excellent audio quality for smartphones, TWS earbuds, smart speakers, and IoT voice applications.

This microphone includes an integrated high-performance ADC, eliminating the need for external analog-to-digital conversion. The PDM interface allows multiple microphones to share a common data line, simplifying multi-microphone arrays for beamforming and noise cancellation applications. The microphone supports clock frequencies from 1.0MHz to 3.25MHz.

The MSD3524D1T features ultra-low power consumption of 160uA, making it ideal for battery-powered devices. The compact 2.75x1.85x0.9mm package enables integration in space-constrained designs. The microphone includes power-down mode with less than 10uA current consumption for power-sensitive applications.
As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for integrating the MSD3524D1T with major audio processors and voice platforms.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.content: The MSD3524D1T is an excellent choice for digital audio applications. The integrated ADC simplifies system design and reduces component count significantly. We've successfully integrated this microphone with Qualcomm, MediaTek, and Cirrus Logic audio platforms without issues. The PDM interface works reliably with multiple microphones on shared data lines - we've tested up to 4 microphones with excellent results. Power consumption is as specified, contributing to good battery life in portable devices. The small package size is perfect for TWS earbuds where space is at a premium. Audio quality is comparable to more expensive digital microphones from established brands.
highlight: Reliable PDM interface for multi-mic arrays

alternativeParts.reason: Analog output version for analog signal chains
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for legacy analog audio systems or when direct analog signal processing is preferred
Use for cost-sensitive digital audio applications where standard 60dB SNR is sufficient

faqs.question: How many MSD3524D1T microphones can share one PDM data line?
faqs.answer: Multiple MSD3524D1T microphones can share a common PDM data line using different clock edges. Typically, 2 microphones can share one data line (one on rising edge, one on falling edge). For larger arrays, use multiple data lines or time-division multiplexing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For stereo applications, use 2 microphones on one data line. For multi-mic arrays, plan data line allocation based on your processor's PDM input capabilities.

faqs.question: What clock frequency should I use with MSD3524D1T?
faqs.answer: The MSD3524D1T supports clock frequencies from 1.0MHz to 3.25MHz. Common frequencies are 1.024MHz, 2.048MHz, and 3.072MHz. Higher clock frequencies provide better audio quality but consume more power. Select based on your application's audio quality and power requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2.048MHz for standard applications. Use 3.072MHz for premium audio. Use 1.024MHz for power-sensitive applications with acceptable audio quality.

faqs.question: Does MSD3524D1T support power-down mode?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MSD3524D1T supports power-down mode with less than 10uA current consumption. This enables always-listening voice activation with minimal battery drain. The microphone wakes up quickly when the clock signal is restored.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use power-down mode for always-listening applications. The microphone can be powered down between voice detection events to save battery.

categories.name: Pressure Sensors
categories.description: High-precision MEMS pressure sensors for altitude measurement, weather monitoring, industrial control, and medical applications with excellent accuracy and stability.
categories.longDescription: Memsensing Pressure Sensors deliver high-precision pressure measurement for a wide range of applications. As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance for these advanced pressure sensors. The product range includes barometric pressure sensors for altitude and weather applications, and differential pressure sensors for industrial and medical use. Featuring high accuracy up to ±0.01hPa, wide pressure ranges, and excellent temperature compensation, these sensors are ideal for smartphones, wearables, drones, industrial equipment, and medical devices. The sensors support various interface options including I2C and SPI digital outputs.
categories.selectionGuide: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal pressure sensor based on your application requirements

faqs.question: What types of pressure sensors does Memsensing offer?
faqs.answer: Memsensing offers barometric pressure sensors for atmospheric pressure measurement and altitude calculation, and differential pressure sensors for flow measurement and industrial applications. Barometric sensors typically measure 300-1100hPa range, while differential sensors offer various ranges based on application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose barometric sensors for altitude and weather applications. Select differential pressure sensors for industrial flow measurement and medical applications.

faqs.question: What accuracy levels are available for Memsensing pressure sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing pressure sensors offer accuracy levels from ±0.01hPa to ±0.5hPa depending on the model. High-precision models provide ±0.01hPa accuracy suitable for precise altitude measurement, while standard models offer ±0.1hPa to ±0.5hPa for general applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select accuracy based on your application's precision requirements. Higher accuracy for precise altitude measurement, standard accuracy for general pressure monitoring.

faqs.question: Are Memsensing pressure sensors temperature compensated?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Memsensing pressure sensors include integrated temperature compensation. The sensors feature on-chip temperature sensors and compensation algorithms to maintain accuracy across the operating temperature range. This eliminates the need for external temperature compensation in most applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: The integrated temperature compensation simplifies system design. For extreme temperature applications, verify the sensor's specified temperature range meets your requirements.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for pressure sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing pressure sensors support I2C and SPI digital interfaces. I2C is the most common interface, allowing multiple sensors on the same bus with different addresses. SPI offers higher data rates for applications requiring fast pressure readings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use I2C for most applications to simplify wiring. Select SPI for applications requiring high-speed data acquisition or when I2C address conflicts exist.

faqs.question: Can pressure sensors be used for altitude measurement?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memsensing barometric pressure sensors are ideal for altitude measurement. By measuring atmospheric pressure and applying the barometric formula, altitude can be calculated with resolution down to 10cm using high-precision sensors. These sensors are widely used in smartphones, wearables, and drones for floor detection and navigation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For altitude measurement, select barometric sensors with high accuracy (±0.01hPa) and implement proper altitude calculation algorithms in your firmware.

faqs.question: What is the typical response time of pressure sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing pressure sensors offer fast response times typically less than 10ms for complete conversion. This enables real-time pressure monitoring for dynamic applications such as drone altitude control and industrial process monitoring.
faqs.decisionGuide: The fast response time supports real-time applications. For high-speed monitoring, verify the conversion time meets your application's update rate requirements.

products.name: High-Precision Barometric Pressure Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-precision barometric pressure sensor with ±0.01hPa accuracy for altitude measurement applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MPS25D is a high-precision barometric pressure sensor designed for accurate altitude measurement and weather monitoring applications. Featuring ±0.01hPa accuracy and 300-1100hPa measurement range, this sensor delivers precise pressure readings for smartphones, wearables, drones, and navigation systems.

This sensor incorporates advanced MEMS technology with integrated temperature compensation, maintaining accuracy across -40°C to +85°C temperature range. The I2C interface supports standard and fast modes (up to 400kHz) for easy integration with microcontrollers. The sensor includes FIFO buffer for storing up to 32 pressure measurements, reducing host processor workload.

The MPS25D features ultra-low power consumption of 2.7uA at 1Hz data rate, making it ideal for battery-powered devices. The compact 2.0x2.5x0.95mm package enables integration in space-constrained designs. The sensor supports various operating modes including standard, low-power, and forced conversion modes for flexible power management.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MPS25D including application guidance, calibration procedures, and altitude calculation algorithm support.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.content: The MPS25D has become our preferred pressure sensor for altitude measurement applications. The ±0.01hPa accuracy is impressive - we've achieved altitude resolution of better than 1 meter in real-world testing. The integrated temperature compensation works effectively, maintaining accuracy across the full temperature range. The FIFO buffer is a valuable feature, allowing the host processor to sleep between readings and wake up to collect buffered data. Power consumption is as specified, contributing to excellent battery life in wearable devices. The I2C interface is straightforward to implement, and the sensor responds reliably to commands. Overall, a high-performance sensor at a competitive price point.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost alternative with standard accuracy
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for general weather monitoring and altitude applications where ±0.1hPa accuracy is sufficient

alternativeParts.reason: Differential pressure sensor for flow applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for flow measurement, filter monitoring, and industrial control applications requiring differential pressure sensing

faqs.question: What is the altitude resolution of MPS25D?
faqs.answer: The MPS25D with ±0.01hPa accuracy provides altitude resolution of approximately 10cm at sea level. The resolution varies with altitude due to the non-linear relationship between pressure and altitude. Higher altitudes have slightly reduced resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: For precise altitude measurement, implement proper barometric formulas and consider temperature compensation. The 10cm resolution is suitable for floor detection and precise navigation.

faqs.question: Does MPS25D require calibration?
faqs.answer: The MPS25D is factory calibrated and typically requires no additional calibration for most applications. For high-precision applications, a single-point offset calibration at a known altitude can improve absolute accuracy. The sensor maintains calibration stability over time and temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For standard applications, use the sensor as-is. For precision applications, perform single-point calibration at a known reference altitude.

faqs.question: How do I calculate altitude from MPS25D pressure readings?
faqs.answer: Altitude can be calculated from pressure using the barometric formula: h = 44330 * (1 - (p/p0)^(1/5.255)), where h is altitude in meters, p is measured pressure, and p0 is sea level reference pressure (1013.25hPa). For improved accuracy, use the hypsometric equation that accounts for temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement the barometric formula in your firmware. For best accuracy, use the hypsometric equation with temperature compensation from the sensor's integrated temperature reading.

products.name: Differential Pressure Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-precision differential pressure sensor with ±10kPa range for flow measurement and industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MPD10D is a high-precision differential pressure sensor designed for flow measurement, filter monitoring, and industrial control applications. Featuring ±10kPa measurement range and ±0.05kPa accuracy, this sensor delivers reliable differential pressure readings for medical devices, HVAC systems, and industrial equipment.

This sensor incorporates advanced MEMS technology with excellent linearity and hysteresis characteristics. The differential measurement capability enables precise flow calculation when combined with flow restriction elements. The sensor includes integrated temperature compensation for stable performance across the operating temperature range.

The MPD10D features digital I2C interface for easy integration with microcontrollers and PLC systems. The compact package enables integration in space-constrained medical and industrial devices. The sensor supports continuous measurement mode for real-time monitoring applications.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MPD10D including application guidance, flow calculation support, and system integration assistance.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.content: The MPD10D is an excellent differential pressure sensor for flow measurement applications. The ±0.05kPa accuracy provides precise flow calculation when paired with appropriate flow elements. We've successfully deployed this sensor in medical ventilator applications with excellent results. The linearity is very good across the full pressure range, and the hysteresis is minimal. The I2C interface is straightforward to implement. The sensor responds quickly to pressure changes, enabling real-time flow monitoring. Temperature stability is good, maintaining accuracy in varying environmental conditions. For industrial and medical flow applications, the MPD10D offers excellent performance at a competitive price.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for altitude measurement, weather monitoring, and barometric pressure sensing applications
Use for high-pressure differential applications such as industrial process control and high-flow measurement

companionParts.relationship: Microphone for alarm and status announcements
Temperature sensor for flow temperature compensation

faqs.question: How do I calculate flow rate from MPD10D differential pressure?
faqs.answer: Flow rate can be calculated from differential pressure using the Bernoulli equation or by applying the specific flow coefficient (K-factor) of your flow restriction element. The relationship is typically: Q = K * sqrt(ΔP), where Q is flow rate, K is the flow coefficient, and ΔP is differential pressure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calibrate your flow system with known flow rates to determine the K-factor for your specific flow element. Implement the flow calculation in your firmware.

faqs.question: Can MPD10D be used for liquid pressure measurement?
faqs.answer: The MPD10D is designed for gas pressure measurement. For liquid applications, special consideration is required for media compatibility and pressure port design. Contact our FAE team to discuss liquid pressure measurement requirements and suitable solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For liquid pressure measurement, consult with our FAE team to ensure proper sensor selection and system design for media compatibility.

faqs.question: What tubing connections are recommended for MPD10D?
faqs.answer: The MPD10D requires appropriate pressure port connections based on your application. Standard barbed fittings or compression fittings can be used with flexible tubing. Ensure tubing material is compatible with the measured media and pressure range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select tubing and fittings rated for your application's pressure range and media compatibility. Use flexible tubing to minimize mechanical stress on the sensor package.

categories.name: Inertial Sensors
categories.description: MEMS accelerometers and gyroscopes for motion sensing, vibration monitoring, navigation, and stabilization applications with high precision and reliability.
categories.longDescription: Memsensing Inertial Sensors provide precise motion and orientation measurement for a wide range of applications. As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance for these advanced inertial sensors. The product range includes accelerometers for vibration and motion detection, gyroscopes for rotation sensing, and combined IMU solutions for complete motion tracking. Featuring high resolution, low noise, and excellent temperature stability, these sensors are ideal for smartphones, wearables, drones, industrial equipment, and automotive applications. The sensors support various interface options including I2C and SPI digital outputs.
categories.selectionGuide: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal inertial sensor based on your motion sensing requirements

faqs.question: What types of inertial sensors does Memsensing offer?
faqs.answer: Memsensing offers accelerometers for linear acceleration and vibration measurement, gyroscopes for angular rate sensing, and combined IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) solutions providing both acceleration and rotation data. Accelerometers are available in various ranges from ±2g to ±200g, while gyroscopes offer ranges from ±125dps to ±2000dps.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose accelerometers for vibration monitoring and tilt sensing. Select gyroscopes for rotation and orientation tracking. Use IMU solutions for complete motion tracking and navigation applications.

faqs.question: What measurement ranges are available for accelerometers?
faqs.answer: Memsensing accelerometers offer measurement ranges from ±2g to ±200g. Standard consumer applications typically use ±2g to ±16g ranges, while industrial vibration monitoring may require ±50g to ±200g ranges. Select the minimum range that accommodates your expected acceleration levels for best resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select the smallest range that covers your expected acceleration. Higher ranges provide lower resolution but can handle larger accelerations without saturation.

faqs.question: Are Memsensing inertial sensors temperature compensated?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Memsensing inertial sensors include integrated temperature compensation. The sensors feature on-chip temperature sensors and compensation algorithms to maintain accuracy across the operating temperature range. This ensures stable performance in varying environmental conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: The integrated temperature compensation simplifies system design. For extreme temperature applications, verify the sensor's specified temperature range meets your requirements.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for inertial sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing inertial sensors support I2C and SPI digital interfaces. I2C is suitable for most applications with lower data rate requirements. SPI offers higher data rates for applications requiring fast sampling such as vibration analysis and motion tracking.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use I2C for standard applications. Select SPI for high-speed data acquisition or when multiple sensors share the bus.

faqs.question: Can inertial sensors be used for vibration monitoring?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memsensing accelerometers are ideal for vibration monitoring applications. High-bandwidth accelerometers can detect vibration frequencies up to several kHz, making them suitable for machine condition monitoring, structural health monitoring, and predictive maintenance applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For vibration monitoring, select accelerometers with appropriate bandwidth and measurement range. Consider the frequency range of vibrations you need to detect.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of inertial sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing inertial sensors offer ultra-low power consumption, typically 10-100uA depending on the model and data rate. Low-power modes enable motion detection with minimal battery drain, making them ideal for battery-powered wearables and IoT devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, select sensors with low-power modes and implement intelligent wake-up strategies to minimize power consumption.

products.name: High-Performance 3-Axis Accelerometer
products.shortDescription: High-performance 3-axis accelerometer with ±16g range and 14-bit resolution for motion sensing applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MIR300 is a high-performance 3-axis MEMS accelerometer designed for motion sensing, vibration monitoring, and orientation detection applications. Featuring ±16g measurement range and 14-bit resolution, this accelerometer delivers precise acceleration measurements for smartphones, wearables, industrial equipment, and IoT devices.

This accelerometer incorporates advanced MEMS technology with integrated temperature compensation, maintaining accuracy across -40°C to +85°C temperature range. The I2C and SPI interfaces support flexible system integration. The sensor includes programmable data rates from 1Hz to 4kHz and multiple operating modes for power management.

The MIR300 features ultra-low power consumption of 10uA in normal mode and 2uA in low-power mode, making it ideal for battery-powered devices. The compact 2.0x2.0x0.95mm LGA package enables integration in space-constrained designs. The sensor supports tap detection, free-fall detection, and activity/inactivity detection with programmable thresholds.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MIR300 including application guidance, calibration procedures, and motion detection algorithm support.

faeReview.author: Robert Chen
faeReview.content: The MIR300 is an excellent accelerometer for motion sensing applications. The 14-bit resolution provides good precision for tilt and motion detection. The integrated tap detection and free-fall detection features simplify firmware development - we've implemented these features in multiple wearable projects with excellent results. The selectable measurement ranges are useful for adapting to different application requirements. Power consumption is as specified, contributing to good battery life in fitness trackers. The temperature stability is good, maintaining accuracy across the operating range. The compact package fits well in space-constrained wearable designs. Overall, a reliable and cost-effective accelerometer solution.
highlight: Good 14-bit resolution for precise motion detection
Useful integrated tap and free-fall detection
Low power consumption for battery applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for cost-sensitive motion detection applications where 12-bit resolution is sufficient
Use for complete motion tracking and navigation applications requiring both acceleration and rotation sensing

faqs.question: What is the sensitivity of MIR300 at different ranges?
faqs.answer: The MIR300 sensitivity varies with measurement range: 0.98mg/LSB at ±2g, 1.95mg/LSB at ±4g, 3.91mg/LSB at ±8g, and 7.81mg/LSB at ±16g. Higher ranges provide lower sensitivity (larger mg per LSB) but can measure larger accelerations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select the smallest range that covers your expected acceleration for best resolution. Change range dynamically if your application has varying acceleration requirements.

faqs.question: Does MIR300 support tap detection?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MIR300 includes integrated tap and double-tap detection with programmable thresholds. The tap detection can wake the system from low-power mode, enabling gesture-based user interfaces with minimal power consumption.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use tap detection for user interface gestures. Configure thresholds based on your application's mechanical design and expected tap forces.

faqs.question: How do I calculate tilt angle from MIR300 accelerometer data?
faqs.answer: Tilt angle can be calculated using trigonometry: pitch = atan2(ax, sqrt(ay^2 + az^2)) and roll = atan2(ay, sqrt(ax^2 + az^2)), where ax, ay, az are accelerometer readings. Ensure you use the appropriate sensitivity scaling to convert raw readings to g-force values.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement tilt calculation in your firmware. Consider using complementary filters or sensor fusion with gyroscope data for dynamic tilt measurement.

products.name: 6-Axis Inertial Measurement Unit
products.shortDescription: High-performance 6-axis IMU combining 3-axis accelerometer and 3-axis gyroscope for complete motion tracking.
descriptionParagraphs: The MIA600 is a high-performance 6-axis Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) combining a 3-axis accelerometer and 3-axis gyroscope in a single package. Featuring ±16g acceleration range and ±2000dps angular rate range with 16-bit resolution, this IMU delivers complete motion tracking for drones, robotics, VR/AR devices, and navigation systems.

This IMU incorporates advanced MEMS technology with integrated sensor fusion algorithms, providing accurate orientation estimation without external processing. The I2C and SPI interfaces support high-speed data transfer up to 10MHz. The sensor includes programmable digital filters, configurable data rates, and intelligent power management.

The MIA600 features low power consumption of 5mA in full operation mode and 1mA in low-power mode. The compact 3.0x3.0x0.9mm package enables integration in space-constrained designs. The sensor supports external magnetometer interface for 9-axis sensor fusion and enhanced heading accuracy.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MIA600 including application guidance, sensor fusion algorithms, and system integration assistance.

faeReview.author: Susan Wang
faeReview.content: The MIA600 is an excellent IMU for motion tracking applications. The integrated sensor fusion provides accurate orientation data without requiring complex external algorithms. We've successfully used this IMU in drone flight controllers with excellent results - the attitude estimation is stable and responsive. The 16-bit resolution provides good precision for both acceleration and rotation measurements. The configurable ranges allow optimization for different applications. Power consumption is reasonable for the performance level. The external magnetometer interface is useful for applications requiring heading reference. For robotics and drone applications, the MIA600 offers excellent performance at a competitive price point.
highlight: Stable attitude estimation for drone applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for simple motion detection and vibration monitoring where gyroscope sensing is not required
Use for navigation and heading reference applications requiring magnetic compass functionality

faqs.question: Does MIA600 include sensor fusion algorithms?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MIA600 includes integrated sensor fusion algorithms that combine accelerometer and gyroscope data to provide accurate orientation estimation. The algorithms run on the sensor's internal processor, reducing host processor workload.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use the integrated sensor fusion for orientation data. For custom fusion algorithms, raw sensor data is also available.

faqs.question: Can MIA600 be used with an external magnetometer?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MIA600 supports external magnetometer interface for 9-axis sensor fusion. This enables enhanced heading accuracy for navigation applications. The sensor can synchronize magnetometer data with IMU data for optimal fusion results.
faqs.decisionGuide: Add an external magnetometer for applications requiring absolute heading reference. The MIA900 includes integrated magnetometer if preferred.

faqs.question: What is the output data rate of MIA600?
faqs.answer: The MIA600 supports programmable output data rates from 12.5Hz to 8kHz for the accelerometer and from 12.5Hz to 8kHz for the gyroscope. Higher data rates provide better temporal resolution but consume more power.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select data rate based on your application's motion dynamics. Use higher rates for fast motion tracking, lower rates for power-sensitive applications.

categories.name: Environmental Sensors
categories.description: MEMS-based environmental sensors for temperature, humidity, and gas sensing in HVAC, smart home, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Memsensing Environmental Sensors provide accurate measurement of temperature, humidity, and gas concentrations for environmental monitoring applications. As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance for these environmental sensors. The product range includes temperature sensors, humidity sensors, and gas sensors for air quality monitoring. Featuring high accuracy, fast response times, and excellent long-term stability, these sensors are ideal for HVAC systems, smart home devices, weather stations, and industrial environmental monitoring. The sensors support I2C digital interface for easy integration.
categories.selectionGuide: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal environmental sensor based on your monitoring requirements

faqs.question: What environmental parameters can Memsensing sensors measure?
faqs.answer: Memsensing offers sensors for temperature measurement (-40°C to +125°C), relative humidity (0-100% RH), and gas concentrations including CO2 and VOCs. These sensors can be combined for comprehensive environmental monitoring in HVAC, smart home, and industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select sensors based on the environmental parameters you need to monitor. Combine multiple sensors for comprehensive air quality and comfort monitoring.

faqs.question: What accuracy levels are available for environmental sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing environmental sensors offer high accuracy: ±0.2°C for temperature, ±2% RH for humidity, and ±50ppm for CO2 measurement. These accuracy levels are suitable for most HVAC, comfort monitoring, and air quality applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select accuracy based on your application's requirements. Higher accuracy for precise climate control, standard accuracy for general monitoring.

faqs.question: How fast do environmental sensors respond to changes?
faqs.answer: Memsensing environmental sensors offer fast response times: 2-5 seconds for temperature, 8-15 seconds for humidity (63% response), and <30 seconds for gas sensors. These response times enable real-time environmental monitoring and control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider response time for dynamic environments. Faster response for real-time control applications, standard response for monitoring applications.

faqs.question: Are environmental sensors calibrated from factory?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Memsensing environmental sensors are factory calibrated and ready to use. The sensors maintain calibration stability over time, with typical drift less than 0.05°C/year for temperature and 0.5% RH/year for humidity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Factory calibration is sufficient for most applications. For critical applications, periodic calibration verification may be performed.

faqs.question: Can environmental sensors be used outdoors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing environmental sensors can be used outdoors with appropriate protection. The sensors operate across -40°C to +85°C or +125°C depending on model. For outdoor use, provide protection from direct water exposure and consider solar heating effects on measurements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For outdoor applications, ensure the sensor's temperature range covers expected conditions. Provide weather protection while allowing air circulation for accurate measurements.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of environmental sensors?
faqs.answer: Memsensing environmental sensors offer low power consumption, typically 1-5uA in measurement mode and <1uA in sleep mode. This enables battery-powered environmental monitoring with years of operation on small batteries.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, use low-power modes between measurements. The ultra-low power consumption supports long-term unattended monitoring.

products.name: High-Precision Temperature Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-precision digital temperature sensor with ±0.2°C accuracy and I2C interface for environmental monitoring.
descriptionParagraphs: The MET10D is a high-precision digital temperature sensor designed for accurate temperature monitoring in HVAC, smart home, and industrial applications. Featuring ±0.2°C accuracy and -40°C to +125°C measurement range, this sensor delivers reliable temperature readings for climate control, equipment monitoring, and environmental sensing.

This sensor incorporates advanced MEMS technology with excellent long-term stability, maintaining accuracy over the sensor lifetime. The I2C interface supports standard and fast modes for easy integration with microcontrollers. The sensor includes programmable resolution from 9-bit to 12-bit, allowing trade-off between conversion time and resolution.

The MET10D features ultra-low power consumption of 1.5uA in active mode and 0.5uA in sleep mode, making it ideal for battery-powered devices. The compact 2.0x2.0x0.9mm package enables integration in space-constrained designs. The sensor supports one-shot and continuous conversion modes for flexible power management.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MET10D including application guidance, calibration procedures, and system integration assistance.

faeReview.author: Emily Liu
faeReview.content: The MET10D is an excellent temperature sensor for HVAC and smart home applications. The ±0.2°C accuracy provides precise temperature control for climate systems. The selectable resolution is useful - we use 12-bit for precise control and 9-bit for quick monitoring. The long-term stability has been excellent in our testing, with minimal drift over months of operation. Power consumption is very low, enabling battery-powered thermostats and sensors. The I2C interface is straightforward to implement. The compact package fits well in small sensor nodes. For environmental monitoring applications, the MET10D offers excellent value.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for general temperature monitoring applications where ±0.5°C accuracy meets requirements
Use for climate control and HVAC applications requiring both temperature and humidity measurement

companionParts.relationship: Humidity sensor for complete climate monitoring

faqs.question: What is the conversion time of MET10D at different resolutions?
faqs.answer: The MET10D conversion time varies with resolution: 30ms at 9-bit, 60ms at 10-bit, 120ms at 11-bit, and 240ms at 12-bit. Higher resolution provides better precision but requires longer conversion time.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select resolution based on your application's accuracy and response time requirements. Use 12-bit for precise measurements, 9-bit for fast monitoring.

faqs.question: Does MET10D require calibration?
faqs.answer: The MET10D is factory calibrated and typically requires no additional calibration for most applications. The sensor maintains excellent calibration stability with typical drift less than 0.05°C per year.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use the sensor as-is for standard applications. For critical applications requiring traceable accuracy, periodic calibration verification may be performed.

faqs.question: Can MET10D be used for cold chain monitoring?
faqs.answer: Yes, the MET10D is excellent for cold chain monitoring with its -40°C to +125°C range and ±0.2°C accuracy. The low power consumption enables battery-powered data loggers for long-term shipment monitoring.
faqs.decisionGuide: For cold chain applications, use the MET10D with a data logging system. The wide temperature range covers frozen and refrigerated shipments.

products.name: Combined Temperature and Humidity Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-precision combined temperature and humidity sensor with ±0.3°C and ±2% RH accuracy for climate monitoring.
descriptionParagraphs: The MEH20D is a high-precision combined temperature and humidity sensor designed for accurate climate monitoring in HVAC, smart home, and weather applications. Featuring ±0.3°C temperature accuracy and ±2% RH humidity accuracy, this sensor delivers reliable environmental data for comfort control, air quality monitoring, and weather stations.

This sensor incorporates advanced MEMS technology with integrated temperature compensation for the humidity measurement, ensuring accurate RH readings across the operating temperature range. The I2C interface supports standard and fast modes for easy integration. The sensor includes programmable resolution and measurement modes for flexible operation.

The MEH20D features low power consumption of 2.5uA in measurement mode, making it suitable for battery-powered devices. The compact 3.0x3.0x1.0mm package enables integration in space-constrained designs. The sensor supports both temperature and humidity measurement in a single conversion cycle, reducing overall power consumption.

As an authorized Memsensing distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for the MEH20D including application guidance, calibration procedures, and HVAC control algorithm support.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.content: The MEH20D is an excellent combined sensor for HVAC and climate control applications. Having both temperature and humidity in one package simplifies design and reduces component count. The ±2% RH accuracy is good for comfort control applications - we've used this sensor in smart thermostats with excellent results. The temperature compensation works well, maintaining humidity accuracy across temperature variations. The single I2C transaction for both readings is efficient. Response time is adequate for HVAC control - fast enough for responsive control but not so fast as to cause noise issues. Power consumption is reasonable for battery-powered devices. For climate monitoring applications, the MEH20D offers excellent value.
highlight: Good ±2% RH accuracy for comfort control

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for temperature-only monitoring applications such as cold chain and industrial process control
Use for precision climate control and laboratory applications requiring higher humidity accuracy

companionParts.relationship: Pressure sensor for complete weather monitoring

faqs.question: How does MEH20D handle condensation?
faqs.answer: The MEH20D can temporarily measure 100% RH during condensation conditions. After condensation evaporates, the sensor returns to normal operation. For applications with frequent condensation, ensure adequate ventilation or consider protective filters.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-humidity environments, allow time for sensor recovery after condensation events. Consider sensor placement to minimize direct exposure to condensation.

faqs.question: What is the humidity response time of MEH20D?
faqs.answer: The MEH20D has a humidity response time of 8 seconds for 63% of a step change (tau 63%). Full response to a large humidity change takes approximately 30-40 seconds. This response time is typical for capacitive humidity sensors.
faqs.decisionGuide: The response time is suitable for HVAC and climate control applications. For faster response requirements, consider air flow across the sensor to improve response.

faqs.question: Can MEH20D be used outdoors?
faqs.answer: The MEH20D can be used outdoors with proper protection. The sensor operates across -40°C to +85°C. For outdoor use, provide protection from direct rain and sunlight while allowing air circulation. A protective filter or shield is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: For outdoor applications, install the sensor in a radiation shield to prevent solar heating and provide weather protection. Ensure adequate ventilation for accurate measurements.
